headline: BREAKING: US launches new strikes on Iran near Strait of Hormuz — ceasefire collapses as inflation week begins.
sub: The US military launched fresh strikes against Iran at 5 PM EDT Tuesday (Jun 9) in retaliation for Iran downing a US Apache helicopter off Oman. Explosions hit Bandar Abbas and Qeshm — ports flanking the Strait of Hormuz where shipping is already at 5% of pre-war levels. Oil spiked and the Nasdaq shed 3.5% intraday before recovering to -1%. With May CPI landing Wednesday and Fed rate-hike odds already at 57%, escalation into the world's most critical oil chokepoint is the worst possible backdrop. Stagflation risk is back at maximum — energy re-shock meets a hawkish Fed debut from Warsh on June 17.

events.text: BREAKING — US Strikes Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
events.detail: US launched retaliatory strikes Jun 9 after Iran downed a US Apache helicopter off Oman. Explosions at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm — directly on the Strait of Hormuz. Ceasefire has collapsed. Crude oil spike and shipping disruption risk are acute. Nasdaq -3.5% intraday.

Most critical inflation print of 2026 — energy base effects from Iran war shock in focus; April print was highest in nearly two years. Now even more consequential with fresh Iran escalation re-spiking oil.
Ceasefire broken — US strikes hit ports flanking the strait. Shipping at just 5% of pre-war levels; further disruption could fully close the chokepoint and send crude to $120+.
Producer prices complete the inflation picture ahead of Warsh's June 17 debut presser — energy shock from new strikes will show in the pipeline.
First major central bank move since Iran ceasefire; guidance sets EUR/USD tone and signals global policy direction — now complicated by fresh escalation.
Post-payrolls repricing — watch 10-year yield for further pressure on rate-sensitive growth stocks. New Iran strikes add upside inflation risk.
Household inflation expectations running hotter than forecaster models; renewed Iran escalation will weigh on confidence.

bias: Maximum caution into Wednesday CPI. Fresh US strikes near the Strait of Hormuz have broken the ceasefire and reignited the energy shock risk — this is the worst combo for inflation data week. Hot CPI + oil spike = yields blow out, growth and semis crater; even a cool CPI print may be muted by war premium in crude. Avoid rate-sensitive longs ahead of Warsh's June 17 debut. Energy names (XLE, OXY) and gold are the tactical hedges. Do not fade oil strength until the Strait situation stabilizes.
